text:
Once upon a time, in a cozy little village, there lived a peculiar old owl named Olmecia. Olmecia was wise beyond her years because she had been listening to the wise old owl atop the tree adjacent to the' Big Feathered Friend' garden.

One sunny afternoon, the forest buzzed with chatter as butterflies flitted about. Suddenly, a group of rabbits wearing tiny little hats popped out of nowhere. They wanted to learn owl things. Olmecia listened intently.

"Oh dear," she began. "You're thinking about flowers. Do tell. Anything about owls? I've read their delightful little owl bedtime stories."

With a chuckle, the rabbits mentioned their favorite tale.

"There's one about a wise owl who lived in a pinecone nest."

"Yes! That one!" Olmecaio listened closely. "The owl tells bedtime stories at night. Once, her favorite book? 'The Wise Owl Who Couldn't Hoo-Hoo.' That
